description: Fires when a specific element (e.g. button, form, banner) is present on a page. The trigger runs after the page has fully loaded (DOM is ready). Even if the element is added to the page after initial load (e.g. via JavaScript), the trigger will still detect it and fire. By default the tag fires whenever the selected element is present on any page; add conditions to fire only on specific pages or under specific criteria. See Piwik PRO help: Element presence trigger.

condition_types.description: Fires the tag when the selected element is present on a page

builtin_variable_for_selector.name: Found Elements
builtin_variable_for_selector.description: A variable used in the element presence trigger. It contains the class name of the elements the trigger is set to detect.
builtin_variable_for_selector.usage: Use this variable_id in the condition that defines which element to watch. The condition's value is the CSS selector (e.g. .promo_banner or a class name).

css_selector_conditions_reference.note: The CSS selector condition must use the built-in variable 'Found Elements' (id 7dead2a9-5804-4cdd-8d73-b04d338952e9). The condition's value is the selector (e.g. .promo_banner). The trigger then fires when that element is present, optionally combined with event conditions below.

condition_types.description: Fires when the visitor is on a page with the selected page URL and the selected element is present
Fires when the visitor is on a page with the selected page path and the selected element is present
Fires when the visitor is on a page on the selected domain and the selected element is present
Fires when a specific history event (pushState, replaceState, popState or hashchange) occurs and the selected element is present
Fires when the URL hash matches the selected condition and the selected element is present
Fires when the previous URL hash matches the selected condition and the selected element is present
Fires when the history state matches the selected condition and the selected element is present
condition_types.example: History state is set or History state equals my-state

condition_types.description: Fires when the previous history state matches the selected condition and the selected element is present
Fires when the visitor was directed by the selected internal referrer and the selected element is present
Fires when the visitor was directed by the selected external referrer and the selected element is present
Fires when the visitor was directed by the selected campaign (utm_campaign) and the selected element is present
Fires when traffic source matches and the selected element is present. Values: campaign, organic search, social, referral, direct
Fires when the visitor is a returning visitor and the selected element is present
Any variable can be used to define trigger conditions

multiplicity: Each time new elements are found

when_to_use: Run a tag when a specific element (banner, form, CTA) appears on the page
Track or act on dynamically injected content that may appear after load
Fire tags only when a given selector is present, with optional page/referrer filters
common_use_cases: Tracking when a specific widget or component is on the page
Conditional tag firing based on element presence plus URL or referrer
best_practices: Use a precise CSS selector (e.g. .promo_banner, #signup-form) to target the element
Add Page URL or Page path conditions to avoid firing on every page
Test that the element is present in the DOM when you expect (including after JS injection)

create_trigger.description: Create an element presence trigger. Requires conditions that define the element using the built-in variable 'Found Elements' (variable_id 7dead2a9-5804-4cdd-8d73-b04d338952e9) with value = CSS selector; optionally add page/event conditions.

trigger_type.description: Must be 'element_presence' for element presence triggers

conditions.description: Array of condition objects. Include at least one condition using the built-in variable 'Found Elements' (variable_id 7dead2a9-5804-4cdd-8d73-b04d338952e9) with value = CSS selector (e.g. .promo_banner); optional event conditions for Page URL, Page path, Referrer, History, etc.

update_trigger.description: Update an existing element presence trigger – only editable fields will be processed

conditions.description: Array of condition objects. Must include at least one condition that defines which element to watch using the built-in variable 'Found Elements' (variable_id 7dead2a9-5804-4cdd-8d73-b04d338952e9) with value = CSS selector (e.g. .promo_banner). May include event conditions: Page URL, Page path, Page hostname, History source/fragment/state, Referrer, Campaign, Traffic source, Returning visitor, or variable-based.

structure.variable_id: For element definition use 7dead2a9-5804-4cdd-8d73-b04d338952e9 (Found Elements); for event conditions use the appropriate variable UUID
structure.value: CSS selector or class name (e.g. .promo_banner) when using Found Elements variable

conditions.recommendation: Include one condition with variable_id 7dead2a9-5804-4cdd-8d73-b04d338952e9 (Found Elements) and value = your selector; add Page path or Page URL conditions to limit where the trigger fires.

element_presence_promo_banner.description: Fire when element matching .promo_banner is present (selector provided via variable)

element_presence_with_page_path.description: Fire when element is present and only on product pages

common_mistakes: Using a selector that never appears in the DOM (e.g. typo or wrong class)
Expecting the trigger to fire before DOM ready (it runs after DOM is ready)
Omitting the element-defining condition so the trigger does not know which element to watch

possible_causes: CSS selector does not match any element
Element appears before DOM ready in a way the trigger does not observe
debugging_steps: Verify the selector in browser dev tools (e.g. document.querySelector('.promo_banner'))
Test with fewer or no event conditions
Ensure the element is present after DOM is ready
